title: Why No High Definition Release
wikitext:
[]: Why No High-Definition Release?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Feb 03, 2008 11:13 pm I mean, it must have been shown in theaters in high definition, so why wasn't 1.0 released on Blu-Ray or HD DVD? Plus there's the fact that most things will probably be out in HD in a few years, and DVDs will be obsolete pretty quickly. Yeah, yeah, there's the fact that people will buy them again when they come out again, but GAINAX is already behind the times with this stunt!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 04, 2008 12:31 am Not everyone has a blu-ray or HD-DVD player, and Gainax would just be shooting themselves in the foot. A large number of people would find the cheaper ordinary DVD worth buying for the few extras and other things (Since plenty of people have seen Rebuild already). They wouldn't want a super-expensive blu-ray. Remember, making profit is not simply selling the most expensive thing possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 04, 2008 1:13 am there probably will be an HD release, they're probably releasing the regular DVD first.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 04, 2008 4:33 am That would almost certainly be a very high-end Collector's edition, probably something they'd release when done with all the Rebuild movies.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Feb 04, 2008 9:29 am UrsusArctos wrote: That would almost certainly be a very high-end Collector's edition, probably something they'd release when done with all the Rebuild movies.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Mon Feb 04, 2008 5:29 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Not everyone has a blu-ray or HD-DVD player, and Gainax would just be shooting themselves in the foot. A large number of people would find the cheaper ordinary DVD worth buying for the few extras and other things (Since plenty of people have seen Rebuild already). They wouldn't want a super-expensive blu-ray. Remember, making profit is not simply selling the most expensive thing possible.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Feb 04, 2008 6:08 pm GAINAX, KHARA WHATEVER /Seriously, though. I need to start paying more attention to that.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Feb 05, 2008 4:34 am I say we don't care. An anime on dvd can be as pretty as its conterpart on hd support. I watched again Titan AE on dvd yesterday evening and it was bloody good looking. I don't know if hd is such a big plus for anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 05, 2008 4:48 am Jinroh wrote: I say we don't care. An anime on dvd can be as pretty as its conterpart on hd support. I watched again Titan AE on dvd yesterday evening and it was bloody good looking. I don't know if hd is such a big plus for anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Feb 05, 2008 4:57 am The last anime on dvd really have an excellent quality... And most of the time if you buy a bluray or hddvd you realize that it's nearly a port from the dvd version. It would be nice to find some comparisons.

Yeah, yeah, there's the fact that people will buy them again when they come out again, but GAINAX
